target.title: fix: expand unsafe host env denylist
target.body:
## Summary

- Expands the shared host environment safety policy to reject additional interpreter startup and search-path variables before they reach host command execution.
- Regenerates the macOS host environment policy from the shared JSON source so app and Node-host sanitizers stay aligned.
- Updates focused regression coverage and user-facing docs that enumerate the blocked environment override classes.
- Intentionally does not change the sanitizer control flow; this keeps the existing policy-driven behavior and only expands the denylist data.

AI-assisted: yes.

## Linked context

No public issue linked in this PR body.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Host environment overrides for additional interpreter startup/search-path variables are rejected by the existing node-host `system.run` handler before process execution.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux using the actual `handleSystemRunInvoke` runtime path and a real child process spawn for the safe case.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm exec tsx /tmp/openclaw-system-run-proof-91618.ts`
- Evidence after fix: The blocked request reported `SYSTEM_RUN_DENIED: environment override rejected (blocked override keys: BASHOPTS, FPATH, KSH_ENV, TCLLIBPATH)` with `executed: false`; the safe request reported `executed: true`, `ok: true`, `stdout: "ok\nBASHOPTS_UNSET\n"`, and `exitCode: 0`.
- Observed result after fix: The four risky env overrides are rejected before `system.run` executes, while an ordinary `OPENCLAW_PROOF_SAFE=ok` env override still reaches the spawned process.
- What was not tested: Full gateway-to-paired-node WebSocket round trip and full repository CI were not run locally.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The proof uses the real node-host handler and real process execution in a local checkout, but not a separately paired node service.
- Before evidence: Existing tests did not cover these variables in the shared policy baseline.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/host-env-security.test.ts src/infra/host-env-security.policy-parity.test.ts src/infra/host-env-security.reported-baseline.test.ts`
- `node scripts/generate-host-env-security-policy-swift.mjs --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `scripts/pr review-tests 91618 src/infra/host-env-security.test.ts src/infra/host-env-security.policy-parity.test.ts src/infra/host-env-security.reported-baseline.test.ts`
- `timeout 1800 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
- `pnpm exec tsx /tmp/openclaw-system-run-proof-91618.ts`

Regression coverage was added for dangerous-key classification, inherited sanitization, override sanitization, diagnostics, reported baseline coverage, and generated macOS policy parity.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `Yes`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `Yes`

Highest-risk area: Host command execution environment filtering.

Risk mitigation: The change reuses the existing denylist policy mechanism, keeps ordinary credential/proxy/server-specific variables unaffected, adds focused tests for the expanded policy surface, and includes runtime proof that safe env overrides still pass.

## Current review state

Next action: Wait for automated CI, ClawSweeper re-review, and maintainer review.

Still waiting on: GitHub CI and any maintainer-requested broader validation.

Bot or reviewer comments addressed: ClawSweeper proof request addressed by adding runtime `system.run` handler proof to this PR body.
target.author: pgondhi987
